propositions: Ship the contract half of the expand/contract by dropping the legacy single-slot state['signature'] mirror from new SNAPSHOTs while keeping the authoritative kind-keyed state['attestations'] map.
Add a new signing.most_recent_attestation(state) helper that returns the greatest-signed_at record from the attestations map, falling back to state['signature'] only when the map is absent or empty.

Migrate all three mirror readers (signing._record_for_kind kind=None, validate.py:signature_findings, txn.py:_signature_gate) to the helper before dropping the write-side mirror so no reader observes a stripped mirror.
Strip the legacy signature key in compact.py for both normal-compaction and fsck-rebuild paths, gated by the new compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror flag defaulting to false (drop).
Make the drop fully reversible with no code downgrade by setting emit_legacy_signature_mirror=true to re-emit the mirror on next compaction, targeting the 0.7.x line.
Gate the one-way-door drop on an operator-attested readiness condition confirming no pre-attestations clone reading state['signature'] remains live, documented in docs/migrations.md.
